steps.title: Find the parabola's minimum using vertex form
steps.content:
Since the parabola $y = x^2 + 8x + k$ opens upward (positive leading coefficient), it has a minimum value. For no real solutions, this minimum must be above the horizontal line $y = -2.5$. Complete the square:
$$y = (x^2 + 8x + 16) - 16 + k = (x+4)^2 + k - 16$$
The minimum $y$-value is $k - 16$.

steps.title: Set up the no-intersection condition
steps.content:
For the parabola and the horizontal line $y = -2.5$ to have no intersection points, the parabola's minimum must be strictly above the line:
$$k - 16 > -2.5$$
$$k > 13.5$$

steps.title: Find the least positive integer
steps.content: Since $k$ is a positive integer and must satisfy $k > 13.5$, the least value is $k = 14$.
